title: Phalocallis gigantea
intro: Phalocallis gigantea, also known as the Giant Calanthe, is a terrestrial orchid native to tropical regions of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections. Bioactive constituents of Phalocallis gigantea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are believed to contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Phalocallis gigantea is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, with the most common herbal preparations being teas and infusions used to treat various ailments.
